name: Basimilus Iteritas Alia
description: Parameterized digital drum voice. Six-oscillator additive/FM engine with Infinifold wavefolder across three modes (Skin, Liquid, Metal). Pitch encoder, envelope output, and firmware-swappable Alia platform.

controls.name: Pitch
controls.description: Rotary encoder sets fundamental pitch. Press to toggle between coarse semitone steps and fine tuning. Sums with Pitch CV.
controls.detail: encoder · press = coarse/fine · sums with Pitch CV (-2V to +5V)

controls.name: Morph
controls.description: Continuously blends the waveform for all six oscillators through sine, triangle, saw, and square. Knob offsets the CV input.

controls.name: Attack
controls.description: Shapes onset for all oscillators plus noise. CCW mixes in noise, dead center is an analog pop, CW slows attack. Knob offsets CV.
controls.detail: CCW: noise burst · noon: click · CW: slow attack

controls.name: Decay
controls.description: Amplitude decay time for all oscillators. In Liquid mode also sets the pitch envelope length. Knob offsets CV.
controls.detail: short to long · affects pitch env in Liquid

controls.name: Harmonic
controls.description: Harmonic count and decay for the six oscillators. CCW a single partial; moving CW introduces partials 2–6 with lengthening decays.
controls.detail: CCW: 1 partial · CW: 6 partials with long decays

controls.name: Fold
controls.description: Infinifolder amount. Lower three quarters set a folding threshold; top quarter mixes in a signal-following pulse train for gritty buzz.

controls.name: Spread
controls.description: Frequency spacing of the five non-fundamental oscillators. Sweeps from strict harmonic series to prime/inharmonic spacings.

controls.name: Mode switch
controls.description: Three-position toggle selects synthesis algorithm. Skin is 6-op additive; Liquid adds a pitch envelope to all partials; Metal phase-modulates two 3-op FM stacks.

controls.name: Range switch
controls.description: Octave range selector. Alto is +2 octaves; Treble is +4 octaves relative to Bass.
controls.detail: Bass · Alto (+2 oct) · Treble (+4 oct) · no CV

controls.name: Hit
controls.description: Momentary button fires the envelope manually. Handy for live hits and auditioning patches without a cable patched to Trig.

inputs.name: Pitch
inputs.description: 1V/oct pitch CV for the fundamental oscillator. Sums with Pitch encoder value.

inputs.name: Morph
inputs.description: CV for waveshape blend. Sums with Morph knob.

inputs.name: Attack
inputs.description: CV for attack shape (noise/click/slow). Sums with Attack knob.

inputs.name: Decay
inputs.description: CV for decay time. Sums with Decay knob.

inputs.name: Harm
inputs.description: CV for harmonic count/decay. Sums with Harmonic knob.

inputs.name: Fold
inputs.description: CV for fold threshold and pulse-train injection. Sums with Fold knob.

inputs.name: Spread
inputs.description: CV for frequency spacing. Sums with Spread knob.

inputs.name: Trig
inputs.description: Rising-edge trigger fires the envelope. Accepts gates, triggers, and audio-rate signals for re-triggered texture.

outputs.name: Out
outputs.description: Main audio output. Low impedance. Internal loudness compensation evens level across parameter changes.

outputs.name: Env Out
outputs.description: Copy of the internal amplitude envelope. Useful for modulating external VCAs, filters, or FX in sync with each hit.

behaviors.name: Skin mode
behaviors.description: Six-operator additive engine with no inter-oscillator interaction. Each partial plays a static ratio set by Spread. Best for kicks, toms, and additive synth stabs. Fold and Harmonic are the primary timbre sculptors since there is no pitch envelope.

behaviors.name: Liquid mode
behaviors.description: Skin architecture plus a pitch envelope applied to all six oscillators. Produces the characteristic 808/909 pitch drop without external patching. Decay scales both amplitude and pitch-envelope length together.

behaviors.name: Metal mode
behaviors.description: Two parallel three-operator phase-modulation stacks. Produces bells, cowbells, crashes, and alien timbres. Spread and Harmonic become FM ratio and index controls; small movements cause large timbral shifts.

behaviors.name: Pitch encoder coarse/fine toggle
behaviors.description: Press action toggles the encoder between coarse semitone steps and fine tuning. Replaces the continuous Pitch pot on the original Alter and allows precise re-tuning without drift. State persists across triggers.

behaviors.name: Envelope output
behaviors.description: Env Out emits 0-5V mirroring the internal amplitude envelope. Patch into an external VCA CV, filter cutoff, or FX parameter so the effect tracks each hit's shape. Alia-only jack; not present on the original Alter.

behaviors.name: Infinifold with pulse injection
behaviors.description: Below 75%, Fold sets a threshold and the engine stacks fold stages with amplitude compensation. Above 75%, a signal-following pulse train is injected at signal extrema for a gritty pitched buzz. Key zone for noisy snares and sub-bass grit.

behaviors.name: Post-fold envelope restore
behaviors.description: After the folder, Alia re-applies a shape derived from the summed oscillators. This preserves percussive dynamics that folding normally flattens, so even maximum Fold retains attack punch instead of becoming a steady drone.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate trig
behaviors.description: Trig accepts audio-rate input. Re-triggering the envelope at oscillator rates produces pitched digital textures, cross-modulated bells, and gritty metallic timbres. Pairs well with Metal mode and high Spread for inharmonic bell sequences.

behaviors.name: Firmware swap
behaviors.description: Alia is a firmware-swappable platform. Rear micro-USB connects to Noise Engineering's Customer Portal to load any other Alia-compatible firmware (Manis, Cursus, Ataraxic, etc.) for free. Only one firmware runs at a time; swap is non-destructive.

behaviors.name: Pitch CV range extended
behaviors.description: Alia accepts -2V to +5V on Pitch CV (seven-octave span). Wider and signed compared to Alter's 0-8V range, so negative voltages from attenuverters and bipolar LFOs track musically without DC offset tricks.

behaviors.name: Autocalibration on boot
behaviors.trigger: power up with Pitch CV unpatched
behaviors.description: On startup with nothing patched into Pitch CV, Alia runs an autocalibration routine for its oscillators. Leaves tracking tight across the full -2V to +5V pitch range. Skipped if a cable is present at boot.

patchIdeas.name: Classic 909 kick
patchIdeas.patch: Liquid mode provides the pitch bloom of a 909 kick from a single module, clocked straight in.
steps: Flip the Mode switch to Liquid.
Flip the Range switch to Bass.
Set Pitch encoder around 9 o'clock-equivalent low tuning, Decay to 2 o'clock, Attack to noon.
Set Morph to 10 o'clock, Harmonic to 10 o'clock, Fold to 10 o'clock.
Turn Spread fully CCW for strict harmonic series.
Patch your clock t> Basimilus Iteritas Alia Trig.
Patch Basimilus Iteritas Alia Out -> your drum mixer.
patchIdeas.hear: A punchy kick with a short pitch drop. Lower Decay for a tighter 606 thump; raise Fold past noon for added drive on the body.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Basimilus Iteritas Alia:
* Mode: Liquid · Range: Bass
* Pitch: low · Decay: 2 o'clock · Attack: noon · Morph: 10 o'clock · Harmonic: 10 o'clock · Fold: 10 o'clock · Spread: full CCW

Clock:
Mixer:

- Clock (Out) t> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Trig)
- Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Metal crash with morphing ratios
patchIdeas.patch: Modulates Spread and Harmonic so every Metal-mode hit lands on a different FM ratio, giving a single voice a library of crashes.
steps: Flip Mode to Metal, Range to Treble.
Set Pitch to noon, Decay to 3 o'clock, Attack to noon.
Patch a slow triangle LFO >> Alia Spread CV.
Patch a stepped random (S&H) >> Alia Harm CV.
Patch a gate sequencer t> Alia Trig.
Patch Alia Out -> your drum mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Out — audio, wash of metallic partials.
patchIdeas.hear: Each hit has a different bell/crash spectrum. Triangle LFO slowly shifts the character between hits; S&H scrambles harmonic count per trigger.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Basimilus Iteritas Alia:
* Mode: Metal · Range: Treble
* Pitch: noon · Decay: 3 o'clock · Attack: noon

LFO:
S&H:
Gate Seq:
Mixer:

- LFO (Tri) >> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Spread)
- S&H (Out) >> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Harm)
- Gate Seq (Out) t> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Trig)
- Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Pulse-train snare
patchIdeas.patch: Pushes Fold past the pulse-injection threshold and uses the Attack noise blend to build a gritty digital snare.
steps: Mode: Skin · Range: Alto.
Set Pitch around 10 o'clock, Decay 10 o'clock, Attack 9 o'clock (noise blend).
Set Morph to 3 o'clock, Harmonic to 1 o'clock, Spread to noon.
Turn Fold fully CW to engage the pulse-train buzz.
Patch your clock t> Alia Trig.
Patch a MATHS CH 1 envelope (triggered by the same clock) >> Alia Attack for velocity-scaled noise.
Patch Alia Out -> your drum mixer.
patchIdeas.hear: A buzzy 606-style snare whose noise-to-pitch balance follows the MATHS envelope. Shorten MATHS Rise for quicker snap.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Basimilus Iteritas Alia:
* Mode: Skin · Range: Alto
* Pitch: 10 o'clock · Decay: 10 o'clock · Attack: 9 o'clock · Morph: 3 o'clock · Harmonic: 1 o'clock · Fold: full CW · Spread: noon

MATHS:
* CH 1: envelope

Clock:
Mixer:

- Clock (Out) t> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Trig)
- Clock (Out) t> MATHS.CH 1 (Trig)
- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Attack)
- Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Env Out ducks the mix
patchIdeas.patch: Uses Alia's Env Out to sidechain-duck another audio bus in time with every kick.
steps: Dial in a kick patch (Liquid, Bass, low Pitch, medium Decay).
Patch clock t> Alia Trig.
Patch Alia Out -> your drum mixer channel.
Patch Alia Env Out >> a VCA CV input with the attenuverter inverted (so +env lowers gain).
Route your pad/bass bus through that VCA.
Tune Alia's Decay knob until the duck length feels right.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Env Out — 0-5V envelope tracking each kick.
patchIdeas.hear: The pad/bass bus dips on every kick and recovers as the kick tail ends. Longer Alia Decay = slower release on the duck.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Basimilus Iteritas Alia:
* Mode: Liquid · Range: Bass

Clock:
VCA:
* Atten: inverted
Pad Bus:
Mixer:

- Clock (Out) t> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Trig)
- Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Env Out) >> VCA (CV) // inverted = duck
- Pad Bus (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Pitched melodic voice
patchIdeas.patch: Uses sustained triggers and Decay full CW to turn Alia into a six-partial additive drone voice tracked by 1V/oct.
steps: Mode: Skin · Range: Alto.
Turn Decay fully CW (long/sustained), Attack around 1 o'clock, Harmonic 11 o'clock, Fold 10 o'clock.
Turn Morph to 9 o'clock (near-sine) and Spread fully CCW (strict harmonic).
Patch a quantized sequencer p> Alia Pitch CV.
Patch a sustained gate (sequencer gate out) g> Alia Trig.
Patch Alia Out -> your main mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Out — pitched additive voice, tracks 1V/oct across -2V to +5V.
patchIdeas.hear: A soft six-partial organ-like tone that follows the sequence. Open Harmonic for a brighter chord-ish timbre; close it for pure sine.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Basimilus Iteritas Alia:
* Mode: Skin · Range: Alto
* Decay: full CW · Attack: 1 o'clock · Harmonic: 11 o'clock · Fold: 10 o'clock · Morph: 9 o'clock · Spread: full CCW

Sequencer:
Mixer:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Pitch)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Trig)
- Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate FM bell
patchIdeas.patch: Drives Trig at audio rates in Metal mode for a re-triggered digital bell sequence.
steps: Mode: Metal · Range: Treble.
Set Pitch high (around 3 o'clock), Decay 2 o'clock, Harmonic full CW, Fold full CW.
Set Spread at 2 o'clock for between-positions aliasing character.
Patch a square-wave VCO (~50 Hz) t> Alia Trig.
Patch a slow sequencer or LFO p> that VCO's V/Oct for bell melody.
Patch Alia Out -> your mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Out — pitched metallic bell with re-triggered FM grain.
patchIdeas.hear: A gritty bell tone whose pitch follows the VCO melody and whose grain tracks the audio-rate retrigger. Lower the VCO frequency for a buzzier timbre.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Basimilus Iteritas Alia:
* Mode: Metal · Range: Treble
* Pitch: 3 o'clock · Decay: 2 o'clock · Harmonic: full CW · Fold: full CW · Spread: 2 o'clock

VCO:
* Wave: Square · ~50 Hz

LFO:
Mixer:

- VCO (Out) t> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Trig) // audio-rate retrigger
- LFO (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct) // bell melody
- Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Env-synced filter bark
patchIdeas.patch: Sends Alia's Env Out into an external filter's cutoff so each kick opens the filter on a separate synth line.
steps: Dial Alia to a short Liquid-mode kick (Bass, Decay ~10 o'clock).
Patch clock t> Alia Trig.
Patch Alia Env Out >> Filter Cutoff CV input.
Route a separate VCO -> that Filter input (not Alia's audio).
Patch Filter Out -> Mixer.
Patch Alia Out -> Mixer too, so both are audible.
Adjust Filter base cutoff so the bark lands in the right register.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Env Out — 0-5V envelope · drives external filter cutoff.
patchIdeas.hear: The external VCO gets a percussive 'bark' every time Alia fires, like a kick-triggered filter accent. Longer Alia Decay = longer filter sweep.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Basimilus Iteritas Alia:
* Mode: Liquid · Range: Bass
* Decay: 10 o'clock

Clock:
VCO:
Filter:
Mixer:

- Clock (Out) t> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Trig)
- Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Env Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Swept inharmonic drone
patchIdeas.patch: Holds a gate high and sweeps Spread between strict positions for a slowly morphing inharmonic drone.
steps: Mode: Skin · Range: Bass.
Decay fully CW, Attack 1 o'clock, Harmonic full CW, Fold 11 o'clock.
Set Morph to noon, Pitch around 10 o'clock.
Patch a slow sine LFO (~20s cycle) >> Alia Spread CV.
Patch a sustained +5V gate g> Alia Trig (or hold the Hit button briefly to latch).
Patch Alia Out -> reverb -> mixer.
patchIdeas.hear: A six-partial drone whose overtones drift between clean harmonic and prime-inharmonic spacings as the LFO moves. Strongest effect is around Spread noon.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Basimilus Iteritas Alia:
* Mode: Skin · Range: Bass
* Decay: full CW · Attack: 1 o'clock · Harmonic: full CW · Fold: 11 o'clock · Morph: noon · Pitch: 10 o'clock

LFO:
Gate Source:
Reverb:
Mixer:

- Gate Source (Out) g> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Trig) // sustained
- LFO (Sine) >> Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Spread)
- Basimilus Iteritas Alia (Out) -> Reverb (In)
- Reverb (Out) -> Mixer (In)
